title: After the Stars owner acceptance and defect-only follow-up
model: Use an efficient reviewer context with medium reasoning; use a coding context only if a defect is confirmed
prompt: Review the deployed After the Stars quest without reimplementing it. Do not read archive/. Automated progression, save, reward, slot-contention, and pre-rebirth concealment checks already pass. When the owner's normal save has completed its first Starry Rebirth, verify the compact Orders tracker, the final Rowan delivery and brief completion card, Dawnthread Workshop selection, switching back to Midnight, and selecting Dawnthread again. Use 390x844 and 360x740 evidence only for any confirmed layout defect. Make no code change unless the review finds a reproducible defect.
acceptance: The tracker and three order cards remain clear after rebirth
The final completion card is readable before it collapses
Dawnthread Workshop unlocks, can be selected, and can be reversed without changing economy
Any confirmed defect passes npm.cmd run check and targeted phone-size browser QA before merging

title: Storage resilience and truthful save feedback
prompt: Do not read archive/. Run npm.cmd run handoff, then work only on Task 16. Inspect AGENTS.md, app.js, platform-adapters.js, audio-feedback.js, platform-adapters.test.cjs, audio-feedback.test.cjs, release-lifecycle.test.cjs, save-compatibility.test.cjs, package.json, and only other active files directly required by the implementation. Objective: ensure failures in the game's existing local-storage boundaries cannot crash normal play or falsely report that a manual save succeeded. Guard acquisition of browser storage plus current save-related getItem, setItem, and removeItem paths against unavailable or throwing storage. Preserve safe startup and normal in-memory play when persistence is unavailable. Return or propagate an accurate save result: show Workshop saved only after a confirmed successful write; keep the future-version protection result distinct; and show a clear, non-destructive message when storage cannot be written. A failed later write must leave the last valid stored save intact. Preserve current successful saves, gameplay schema migration, future-version overwrite protection, rollback compatibility, platform state, and audio preference behavior. Add deterministic tests for throwing or unavailable storage, including read, write, and relevant removal paths and preservation of a prior valid value. Prefer the smallest testable change within existing storage boundaries; add no dependency, remote service, account, cloud save, analytics, or new production subsystem. Do not edit AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, coder-tasks.json, any other documentation, release files, or archive/. Do not commit or push. Explicitly out of scope: hostile numeric normalization; above-level active-brew recovery; daily rollover; achievement or lifetime-coin accounting; idle or Stardust economy changes; Welcome Back redesign; mobile action hierarchy; narrative or content additions; release, monetization, accounts, analytics, cloud saves, or native platform work. Run npm.cmd run check. Test manual Save Now at 390x844 and 360x740 with no horizontal overflow and check browser console errors and warnings. Finish with STATUS: PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED, then report summary, changed files, validation evidence, assumptions, and deferred or out-of-scope findings. Do not start another task.
acceptance: Throwing or unavailable storage does not crash startup or normal play
Failed manual saves never display a success result
Successful manual saves retain the current success behavior
A failed write does not erase or replace the last valid save
Future-version overwrite protection still passes
Existing migrations and rollback-reader compatibility still pass
Deterministic tests cover storage read, write, and relevant removal failure behavior
npm.cmd run check passes
Manual Save Now is tested at 390x844 and 360x740 with no horizontal overflow
Browser console errors and warnings are checked
The coder reports changed files, assumptions, validation, deferred findings, and one PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED status

title: Truthful Welcome Back recap
prompt: Do not read archive/. Run npm.cmd run handoff, then work only on Task 17. Inspect AGENTS.md, app.js, verify.cjs, package.json, and only other active files directly required by this correction. Objective: make the existing Welcome Back modal truthful about offline ingredients that have already been granted before the modal opens. Update its body to state clearly that the reported ingredients were added to the Pantry while the player was away. Replace the claim-like Collect ingredients action with a non-claiming dismiss or return action such as Back to workshop. The action must not grant ingredients again or introduce a second collection step. Preserve the existing modal timing and all offline elapsed-time, first-delivery, four-hour cap, Pantry soft-cap, random grant, lifecycle, save, and economy behavior. Add a focused deterministic or static contract check that protects the already-applied wording and non-claiming action from regression. Prefer the smallest change; do not create a new reward, claim state, currency, timer, modal system, dependency, or production subsystem. Do not edit AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, coder-tasks.json, any other documentation, release files, or archive/. Do not commit or push. Explicitly out of scope: idle-return tuning; Stardust; hostile saves; active-brew recovery; daily rollover; progression accounting; mobile action hierarchy; narrative or content additions; release, monetization, accounts, analytics, cloud saves, or native work. Run npm.cmd run check. Exercise the Welcome Back modal at 390x844 and 360x740, confirm dismissing it does not change the already-granted ingredient count, confirm no horizontal overflow, and check browser console errors and warnings. Finish with STATUS: PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED, then report summary, changed files, validation evidence, assumptions, and deferred or out-of-scope findings. Do not start another task.
acceptance: The Welcome Back body says the reported ingredients were already added to the Pantry
The primary action is a non-claiming dismiss or return action rather than Collect ingredients
Dismissing the modal does not grant ingredients again
Offline timing, first-delivery, four-hour cap, Pantry soft-cap, lifecycle, save, and economy behavior are unchanged
A focused contract check protects the truthful body and action wording
npm.cmd run check passes
The modal is checked at 390x844 and 360x740 with no horizontal overflow
Browser console errors and warnings are checked
The coder reports changed files, assumptions, validation, deferred findings, and one PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED status

title: Foreground daily rollover correctness
prompt: Do not read archive/. Run npm.cmd run handoff, then work only on Task 18. Inspect AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, game-logic.js, app.js, game-logic.test.cjs, package.json, and only other active files directly required by this correction. Objective: make Daily Goal rollover correct in a game that remains open across local midnight. The current flow can credit the first post-midnight delivery to the previous saved day and then erase it during render, or allow a completed previous-day goal to be claimed before render resets it. Ensure a successful order fulfillment resets a genuinely older saved daily date before incrementing daily progress, using the action's supplied time consistently. Ensure a Daily Goal claim resets the date before testing eligibility, so a stale previous-day completion cannot grant coins, Stardust, or a special-request invitation. Preserve same-day fulfillment and claim behavior and the existing monotonic saved-date high-water rule: clock rollback or returning to an already-saved date must not reopen or erase progress. Add a foreground transition check so an open visible session refreshes and schedules persistence when the local date advances even if the player takes no action; do not cause a full render or save every tick. Keep all values and state shape unchanged, and do not bump the save version. Add deterministic clock-based tests for the midnight delivery, stale claim, same-day path, repeated calls, and clock rollback. Prefer the smallest testable change. Do not edit AGENTS.md, GAMEPLAY_ROADMAP.md, coder-tasks.json, any other documentation, release files, or archive/. Do not commit or push. Explicitly out of scope: Daily Goal threshold or reward changes; streaks; Stardust tuning; rolling-request changes; progression accounting; hostile numeric saves; invalid active brews; idle tuning; mobile hierarchy; narrative or other content; release, monetization, accounts, analytics, cloud saves, or native work. Run npm.cmd run check. Check the Daily Goal presentation at 390x844 and 360x740 with no horizontal overflow and inspect browser console errors and warnings. Do not wait for real midnight or mark the task blocked solely because browser clock control is unavailable; deterministic supplied-time tests are the required rollover evidence. Finish with STATUS: PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED, then report summary, changed files, validation evidence, assumptions, and deferred or out-of-scope findings. Do not start another task.
acceptance: A previous-day 4-of-5 goal followed by one successful post-midnight delivery becomes a current-day 1-of-5 goal
A completed unclaimed previous-day goal cannot grant coins, Stardust, or an invitation after the local date advances
Same-day fulfillment and claiming retain current behavior and rewards
Repeated rollover checks are idempotent and clock rollback cannot reopen or erase the saved high-water date
A visible foreground date transition refreshes and schedules persistence once without full-render or save churn every tick
Daily values, rolling requests, save shape, migrations, and economy remain unchanged
Deterministic supplied-time tests cover the midnight, stale-claim, same-day, repeated-call, and rollback paths
npm.cmd run check passes
The Daily Goal presentation is checked at 390x844 and 360x740 with no horizontal overflow
Browser console errors and warnings are checked
The coder reports changed files, assumptions, validation, deferred findings, and one PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED status
